title: containers will not start with btrfs storage - v4.0-rc4
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->
<!--
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**
Unable to start a container using Podman 4.0-rc4 when storage driver is set to btrfs, this was working without issue on 3.4.4.  All file systems on host are btrfs.

Start of container fails with setxattr operation not permitted 

Problem occurs with both rootless and rooted containers.

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. on clean system set driver = "btrfs" in the [storage] section of $HOME/.config/contatainer/storage.conf

2.podman run --rm -it registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:35

3.

**Describe the results you received:**

Error: error creating container storage: error creating read-write layer with ID "4c1a63bd8b1635d61ad3b6a3d9d51034c859459d64f3dc7c47354b75362da7dc": setxattr /home/steve/.local/share/containers/storage/btrfs/subvolumes/4c1a63bd8b1635d61ad3b6a3d9d51034c859459d64f3dc7c47354b75362da7dc/etc/alternatives/libnssckbi.so.x86_64: operation not permitted

**Describe the results you expected:**
container to start and present bash shell

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

similar error happens with rooted as well as rootless.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.0.0-rc4
API Version:  4.0.0-rc4
Go Version:   go1.16.13

Built:      Fri Feb  4 15:25:35 2022
OS/Arch:    linux/amd64
```

Yes

comments.body:
the btrfs backend is not well maintained as the overlay which is the default we use.

I'd suggest using overlay, as it has more features and it is actively maintained by the core developers.

Any particular reason for using btrfs?

At the same time, we can't break existing btrfs installs.

Is there a possibility this is an environment issue? Or is this affecting all btrfs systems?

@giuseppe I currently using btrfs as it performs slightly better fuse-overlayfs for rootless containers as its all stays in kernel space rather then user space assuming you accept the risk associated with allowing users to delete sub volumes, i'm also using volume snapshots for backups these snapshots do not cross sub volume boundaries.  Have switched to overlay for the moment.

@mheon will check on another system or spin up a vm to check later this week, but this was working happily with v3.4.4 before i upgraded, is the setting of extended attributes new in v4?  i can set extended attributes on the command line without problems on this system.
Overlay native is supported in rootless mode on recent kernels.  Even on RHEL8.5, it is supposed to work.

@mheon sorry for the tardiness trying this elsewhere, have just spun up a clean f35 vm with a btrfs file system, performed dnf upgrade the installed podman 4.0rc4 from copr, created .config/containers/storage.conf with driver=btrfs.  get the same error on a clean build.  so thinking it's not a config problem.

@rhatdan was not aware of that will have a look.
I overcome this problem by installing `btrfs-progs-devel`.
I think this is resolved so closing.  Reopen if I am mistaken.
